title: The War Runs on End-Times Scripts
subtitle: A cleaned reading of Game Theory #12
voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Interpretive claims stay tied to exact source spans.
dek: Jiang makes the Iran war a test of religious prediction: if Al-Aqsa survives and peace arrives, his model fails. If the shrine falls, the deeper engine is not realpolitik but converging apocalypse scripts.
thesis: The lecture argues that the Iran war cannot be explained by budgets, deterrence, or ordinary state interest alone. Those explanations miss the story-layer that makes actors move. Eschatology is not private belief here; it is a social operating system, a script that gives people roles, sacrifice, timing, enemies, and an end state. When Jewish, Christian Zionist, Islamic, Catholic, Orthodox, and Persian end-times scripts overlap, Jiang says they form a predictive map. Their convergence points are stark: Third Temple, Greater Israel, Pax Judaica, Gog and Magog, the decline of the United States and China, and a civil war that removes America from the future scene.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: This is a lecture built as a wager. Jiang opens by restating three predictions about the Iran war: U.S. ground troops and a national draft, no tactical nuclear weapons, and the destruction of the Al-Aqsa/Dome of the Rock site. The first clips are not decoration. Blumenthal shows the official war story has no budget, no endpoint, and already points toward ground troops. The Iranian interview reverses the fear assumption: Iran is not terrified of invasion; it is waiting for it. Then the religious material arrives. Schneerson tells Netanyahu to hurry history toward the Messiah. Netanyahu reads about Rome and says the next war cannot be lost. Christian Zionists call the modern Israeli sequence a series of miracles whose next step is the Temple. An Israeli rabbi imagines a false-flag shrine destruction. By the time Jiang names the law of eschatological convergence, the argument is already moving: the war is not just fought by armies. It is fought by stories that know where history is supposed to end.

marks.text: This is a lecture built as a wager.
Iran is not terrified of invasion; it is waiting for it.
hurry history toward the Messiah
stories that know where history is supposed to end

beats.heading: The Prediction Has a Falsifier
beats.summary: Jiang frames the war through three predictions and makes Al-Aqsa the test of the whole model.

paragraphs.text: The opening does not hide the stakes. Jiang says the United States will use ground troops, ground troops will mean a national draft, and Israel and the United States will not use tactical nuclear weapons. The third prediction is the dangerous one: Al-Aqsa will be destroyed during this war. He later sharpens it into a falsifier. If the site is not destroyed and peace is achieved, then the predictive model is wrong and he should be ignored. That gives the episode its pressure. The shrine is not only a topic; it is the exposed wire of the argument.

marks.text: The shrine is not only a topic; it is the exposed wire of the argument.

paragraphs.text: The war clips establish the surface evidence. Blumenthal emerges from a congressional briefing angry that costs, objectives, troop deployments, and Russian aid to Iran remain unclear. Jiang reads that as an open-ended war already moving toward U.S. ground deployment. The Iranian interview makes the reversal harder to ignore. Asked about American boots on the ground, the Iranian side says it is waiting. Jiang hears a trap: Washington has not seriously considered losing, because Washington has become a bubble where everyone believes the same thing.

marks.text: Washington has become a bubble

beats.heading: Rome Is Not Iran
beats.summary: Netanyahu, Christian Zionist miracle language, and the false-flag rabbi clip move the lecture from geopolitics into sacred geography.

paragraphs.text: The Schneerson clip gives the war a clock. Netanyahu is presented as an up-and-coming politician asking a powerful rabbi for blessing. The rabbi grants support but says things are not moving fast enough: the Messiah must come, and events can be set in motion to force that coming. Then Netanyahu appears in another clip reading about Jews against Rome. The obvious historical enemy is ancient Rome, but Jiang asks who Rome is now. Not Iran, probably not Russia. Rome is probably America, the great empire. That is the first full reversal: the ally in the visible war may be the enemy in the sacred script.

marks.text: the ally in the visible war may be the enemy in the sacred script

paragraphs.text: The Temple clips make the mechanism concrete. A Christian Zionist in Jerusalem calls 1917, 1948, 1967, and 2017 miracles and asks why the reestablishment of the Temple should be impossible. Jiang reads the sequence as a divine plan whose next miracle requires the Third Temple. But the Third Temple has a location problem: Al-Aqsa is there. Then the Israeli rabbi clip supplies an ugly operational fantasy. During Iranian missile fire, one could pretend an Iranian missile hit the Dome of the Rock, set Arabs against Persians, and clear the place. The religious angle is no longer abstract. It has a target, a method, and a war.

marks.text: It has a target, a method, and a war.

beats.heading: Story Is the Operating System
beats.summary: The named law arrives: eschatology answers the human purpose questions and turns believers into coordinated actors.

paragraphs.text: Eschatology means a story of how the world ends, but Jiang does not treat it as theology trivia. It answers the three human questions: where we come from, why we are here, and where we are going. Because it answers those questions, it tells people what role they play. The law of eschatological convergence follows from that: compare the scripts, find where they overlap, and those overlap points become predictive. A story is the operating system of a society. People act it out before they fully know they are acting it out.

marks.text: A story is the operating system of a society.

paragraphs.text: The game-theory layer explains why stories matter. Power is mass times energy times coordination. Raw numbers help, but coordination matters most, and energy matters more than mass. Leaders can coordinate. Families can coordinate. But leaders die and families split. Narrative lasts longer because it implants a script. Eschatology is the strongest narrative because it can cross centuries and borders. It tells people what sacrifice means, when history is late, and why violence can feel like obedience.

marks.text: Power is mass times energy times coordination.
violence can feel like obedience

paragraphs.text: That is why the extreme version cannot be dismissed as fringe. Mainstream Jewish redemption waits for humility, repentance, and God. The accelerationist version says waiting is irresponsibility: create Israel, gather the diaspora, build the Third Temple, defeat the enemies, and the Messiah will come. Most people may call this heresy, but Jiang says the extreme script becomes a vector. The people who believe it work hardest, so they pull the mainstream toward them.

marks.text: the extreme script becomes a vector

beats.heading: A Network Above the State
beats.summary: The Chabad rescue story becomes Jiang's concrete example of eschatological coordination crossing money, politics, enemies, and wartime bureaucracy.

paragraphs.text: The long Chabad-Lubavitch rescue story is not a detour. It is an example of coordination. Jiang presents the movement as small, global, resource-pooled, politically connected, and driven by a shared eschatological vision. In the 1939 Warsaw story, American lawyers, Brandeis-linked channels, the State Department, German contacts, military intelligence, and officers inside Nazi structures become part of one rescue chain. The point is not that the names are clean in the ASR; many are not. The point is the pattern: a religious network can coordinate above the visible bureaucracy of nation-states.

marks.text: a religious network can coordinate above the visible bureaucracy of nation-states

beats.heading: The Scripts Line Up
beats.summary: Jiang maps Persian, Jewish, Christian Zionist, Islamic, Catholic, and Orthodox scripts until their convergence points appear.

paragraphs.text: The taxonomy begins with Zoroastrian light against darkness, judgment, and heaven on earth. Jiang uses it to read Iranian restraint: do not become the villain, fight for the light, wait for judgment. Then Jewish eschatology appears as redemption, Messiah, Third Temple, and Gog and Magog. Christian Zionism borrows that script and twists it: the Jewish Messiah becomes the Antichrist, Jesus returns, and Christians are raptured. The current alliance is therefore temporary. Zionists and Christian Zionists work together toward the Great Ritual Project, then their stories diverge and fight.

marks.text: the Jewish Messiah becomes the Antichrist
their stories diverge and fight

paragraphs.text: The Freemasonic strand turns the Antichrist into a system: Pax Judaica, a Jerusalem-based one-world government, AI surveillance, digital ID, digital currency, and the Mark of the Beast. Islamic eschatology responds with Dajjal and, in the Shia Iranian version, the Mahdi leading believers against the Antichrist. Catholic eschatology wants City of God restored after Anglo-American Protestant power falls like Rome. Orthodox eschatology wants Moscow as Third Rome, no Fourth Rome, Russia unifying the Orthodox world, and Greeks returning to Constantinople.

marks.text: turns the Antichrist into a system
no Fourth Rome

paragraphs.text: Once the scripts are on the table, the convergence points become the prediction engine. Greater Israel and Pax Judaica are achieved. Al-Aqsa gives way to the Third Temple. Anti-Semitism rises because diaspora return is needed. Gog and Magog approaches. The United States and China are absent from the end-time map, which means they must become insignificant; for America, Jiang names civil war. The war in Iran is happening, in this reading, because many traditions want their version of heaven on earth and agree on enough of the same steps to move history in one direction.

marks.text: the convergence points become the prediction engine

beats.heading: Intent Does Not Save You
beats.summary: The convergence map becomes a two-to-four-year forecast, then a student objection forces Jiang to distinguish motive from outcome.

paragraphs.text: The quick predictions are brutal. U.S. ground troops create a draft; the draft creates protests; protests bring the National Guard; domestic conflict follows. The United States loses, and CENTCOM transfers to Israel. The GCC is finished, its economies collapsed and made irrelevant. Turkey and Saudi Arabia enter and suffer. Al-Aqsa is destroyed. Persia rises because the Islamic world must unite against Israel. Greater Israel and Pax Judaica are achieved, capital and technology flow to Israel, Russia wins in Ukraine, Greeks return to Constantinople, Europe and NATO are destroyed. This is not presented as a mood. It is the output of the convergence map.

marks.text: This is not presented as a mood. It is the output of the convergence map.

paragraphs.text: A student gives the strongest objection: maybe Trump simply tried to do to Iran what he did to Venezuela, failed, and Iran fought back. That would be political, not religious. Jiang grants the possibility. Trump may be stupid, arrogant, panicking after a failed move. But motive is not the same as outcome. Once war starts, it cannot simply be ended. Iran is a mountain fortress; the GCC is exposed desert. If America leaves, Iran can demand compensation, tax the Strait of Hormuz, redirect Gulf money from the U.S. to Iran, rebuild into an economic miracle, and become the Middle East superpower.

marks.text: motive is not the same as outcome
tax the Strait of Hormuz

paragraphs.text: That is the closing move. America cannot go home because its finance, AI, stock market, and investment structure depend on Gulf money. If it leaves, the economy collapses and revolution comes anyway. So whether Trump intended the trap or stumbled into it, the outcome aligns with the eschatologies. The law of eschatological convergence is not only a theory of motive. It is a theory of lock-in: once the right scripts and incentives overlap, even a mistake can start acting like destiny.

marks.text: even a mistake can start acting like destiny

questions.question: Could the America-Iran war be explained politically rather than religiously, as Trump trying and failing to destroy Iran the way he tried with Venezuela?
questions.answer: Jiang says this is a fair explanation for how the war may have begun: Trump may be arrogant, panicked, and losing. But he argues that once the war starts, the outcome no longer depends on pure intention. Iran can exploit GCC vulnerability, Hormuz, compensation demands, and U.S. dependence on Gulf capital, so the same endpoint still aligns with the eschatological convergence map.

source_notes: Several proper names in the transcript are ASR-damaged, especially in the Chabad-Lubavitch rescue passage and some clip attributions. This read preserves the argument those passages support without overcorrecting uncertain names.

This episode contains Jiang's dated 2026-03-12 predictions about the Iran war, Al-Aqsa, U.S. civil conflict, GCC collapse, Pax Judaica, and NATO/Europe. They are presented as source-grounded claims from the lecture, not as validated outcomes.
